page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the ecs.g6 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz with an x86_64 architecture. The instance features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs, with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it operates on a KVM hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a balanced profile with some single-core limitations. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance and single-threaded gzip compression are categorized as weak, placing in the bottom 25%. However, multi-core performance is more robust, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, PassMark CPU score, and PassMark CPU physics tests all scoring in the average range. Memory bandwidth tests, including both cached small-block and uncached large-block reads and writes, consistently perform in the average tier. Database operations, such as Redis SET operations and PassMark database operations, also demonstrate average performance.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.g6.2xlarge offers a balanced resource density of 4 GB of RAM per vCPU, making it a standard option for workloads that require a steady allocation of dedicated compute and memory without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or compute-optimized hardware. The lack of bundled local storage means storage costs are decoupled, requiring external block storage. This instance is suitable for general-purpose workloads, including static web serving, average-demand database operations, and multi-threaded application servers that do not require high single-core burst capabilities.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and 32.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance operates on a KVM hypervisor, provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps, and contains no local storage or GPU hardware. Benchmark data shows average multi-core CPU performance, average memory bandwidth, and average database operations, though single-core performance and single-threaded compression tasks fall into the bottom 25%. Qualitatively, the instance offers a standard resource density for general-purpose workloads. It is best suited for static web serving, moderate database operations, and multi-threaded application hosting where dedicated compute resources are required without specialized hardware overhead.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6.2xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 2 Gbps network baseline, delivering average multi-core and database benchmark performance.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and average multi-core performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual server featuring dedicated x86_64 processors and a standard 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio for steady workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8269CY vCPUs and 32.0 GB of system memory.
Average multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance.
KVM hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.
No bundled local storage or GPU hardware.
Suited for static web serving, multi-threaded applications, and moderate database tasks.
